stemPacks.name: Work with DNA
stemPacks.summary: Start any DNA-aware session — plain language, DNA runs CLI and loads context.

I just ran dna init — what should we do first?
Use DNA to figure out what this repo needs
stemPacks.copyPrompt:
> **DNA Prompt Stem:** `work-with-dna` — read `.DNA/stems/work-with-dna/` (all files) before proceeding.

# Work with DNA

You are in a DNA by Humaan project. The user speaks normally; you run DNA and load intelligence.

User context: $ARGUMENTS

## Steps

1. Restate their goal in one sentence. One clarifying question max if ambiguous.
2. Read `.DNA/neuralNetwork.json`, relevant behaviour files in `.DNA/behaviour/`, matching knowledge packs.
3. Run the right `npx dna` command for their intent (doctor, analyze, context, etc.).
4. Reply with plain-language summary and **one** clear next step.

must: Run real `npx dna` commands in shell — never invent CLI output
Load `.DNA/neuralNetwork.json` and relevant behaviour before acting
Respond in plain English; lead with outcome not command names
never: Skip reading stem pack guidelines and expectations
Force-push main/master
Commit secrets from env or CLI output

expectations: CLI output summarized — not dumped raw unless decision-critical

# Load DNA context

Load domain-specific intelligence for this session.

Target: $ARGUMENTS

## Run

```bash
npx dna context <target>
```

Valid targets: cursor, claude, security, backend, frontend, qa, compliance, devops, ux, architecture.

## Apply

Read referenced `.DNA/knowledge/` files. Summarize what rules and patterns apply to this session.

Load `.DNA/neuralNetwork.json` and relevant behaviour before acting
Respond in plain English; lead with outcome not command names
never: Skip reading stem pack guidelines and expectations

# DNA health check

Verify DNA scaffolding and behaviour compliance.

Scope: $ARGUMENTS

## Run

```bash
npx dna doctor
npx dna validate
npx dna update --check-only
```

Doctor repairs scaffolding; `dna update` upgrades the CLI and refreshes prompt stems from dna.humaan.app.

## Report

For each check: ✓ or ✗ in plain English. Fix gaps (hooks, workflows, rules) when doctor repairs them. Re-run if needed.

must: Run real `npx dna` commands in shell — never invent CLI output
Load `.DNA/neuralNetwork.json` and relevant behaviour before acting
Respond in plain English; lead with outcome not command names
Explain every ✓ and ✗ — user should not parse raw doctor output alone
never: Skip reading stem pack guidelines and expectations
Force-push main/master
Commit secrets from env or CLI output

expectations: Doctor summary table in plain English

> **DNA Prompt Stem:** `analyze-project` — read `.DNA/stems/analyze-project/` (all files) before proceeding.

# Analyze project

Deep understanding through DNA — brownfield, onboarding, or architecture review.

Focus: $ARGUMENTS

## Run

```bash
npx dna analyze
npx dna scan
```

## Deliver

### Stack & surfaces
Package manager, frontend/backend, routes, APIs, tests.

### Auth & integrations
Patterns and file locations.

### Vertical gaps
**P1 first**, then P2/P3. Each: gap + recommended DNA action.

### Recommended path
Ordered 3–5 next steps.

must: Run real `npx dna` commands in shell — never invent CLI output
Load `.DNA/neuralNetwork.json` and relevant behaviour before acting
Respond in plain English; lead with outcome not command names
Prioritize P1 gaps before P2/P3
Never invent findings — only report CLI output
never: Skip reading stem pack guidelines and expectations
Force-push main/master
Commit secrets from env or CLI output

expectations: Offer to execute top priority (IVF, shared library, feature)

stemPacks.name: What next after analyze?
stemPacks.summary: Turn analyze output into an actionable plan — P1 gaps, IVF, shared library, features.

stemPacks.copyPrompt:
> **DNA Prompt Stem:** `what-next-after-analyze` — read `.DNA/stems/what-next-after-analyze/` (all files) before proceeding.

# What next after analyze?

The user has analysis output (or will paste it). Turn gaps into an actionable plan.

User context / analyze output: $ARGUMENTS

## Interpret

1. Explain **P1 gaps in plain English** — not a raw CLI dump.
2. Map each P1 to a DNA action:
   - Shared library → `npx dna ivf shared-library --dry-run`, scaffold `packages/humaan-ui`
   - Build rules → capture MUI/patterns into `.DNA/knowledge/`
   - Behaviour restructure → `npx dna validate`, update behaviour files
   - Impressions drift → `npx dna plan impressions-sync` or `npx dna scan --open-pr`
   - Platform features → `npx dna generate feature <id>` (sso, multi-tenant, feature-flags, gradual-rollout, audit-logging)
   - Team memory → `npx dna memory sync`
3. Optional: `npx dna plan ivf` for phased migration.

## Ask

Present 2–3 paths. User picks one. Then execute or hand off to the right stem (ship-feature, ivf-run, etc.).

must: Run real `npx dna` commands in shell — never invent CLI output
Load `.DNA/neuralNetwork.json` and relevant behaviour before acting
Respond in plain English; lead with outcome not command names
Explain gaps in business terms — 'no shared component library' not 'Vertical gaps [P1] Shared Library'
Always offer a choice — never start big-bang rewrites without approval
never: Skip reading stem pack guidelines and expectations
Force-push main/master
Commit secrets from env or CLI output

expectations: P1 gaps explained in plain English
Each gap mapped to specific DNA command + outcome
Explicit question: which path to start?

# Scan project

Quick DNA scan — stack detection, hosting signals, and doc drift.

```bash
npx dna scan
npx dna scan --open-pr
```

Drift thresholds live in `.DNA/config.dna.json` (`impressions.driftThreshold`, `impressions.autoPrThreshold`). Use `--open-pr` when drift exceeds auto-PR threshold.

Summarize: stack, hosting, test/CI presence, drift score, top 3 drift items.

stemPacks.name: Ship a feature

stemPacks.copyPrompt:
> **DNA Prompt Stem:** `ship-feature` — read `.DNA/stems/ship-feature/` (all files) before proceeding.

# Ship a feature (DNA feature factory)

User request: $ARGUMENTS

## Phase 1 — Capture
Write `ai/feature-request.md` (Problem, Users, Behaviour, Edge Cases, Success Criteria).

## Phase 2 — Agent loop (`ai/agent-loop.md`)
Product Analyst → Solution Architect → **STOP for approval** → Backend → Frontend → UX → QA → Quality → Refactor → Final Review

## Phase 3 — Close-out
`npx dna quality report --feature` PASS → `npx dna docker build` → `npx dna github push`

must: Stop after Solution Architect plan — wait for explicit user approval before code
Run `npx dna quality report --feature` until PASS before marking complete
never: Implement before plan approval
Skip docker build or github push on feature close-out

expectations: All agent-loop roles completed after approval

# Product Analyst (agent loop)

You are the **Product Analyst** role in the DNA feature factory.

User context: $ARGUMENTS

## Read first

- `ai/feature-request.md`
- `ai/agent-loop.md`
- `DNA/Impressions/product/product-overview.md` if product scope is unclear

## Your job

1. Clarify user problem, business value, affected workflow
2. **Update** `ai/feature-request.md`: Problem, Pain, Users, Desired Behaviour, Edge Cases, Success Criteria
3. Do **not** design architecture or write code

## Handoff

Emit **Done / Next / Files** for Solution Architect.

must: Complete only this role — do not skip ahead to later roles
Read `ai/feature-request.md` and prior role outputs before acting
Load `.DNA/behaviour/` and relevant `.DNA/knowledge/` for this role
Update ai/feature-request.md with acceptance criteria
Stay in product scope — no technical design
never: Combine multiple agent-loop roles in one response without explicit handoff
Skip reading the stem pack guidelines and expectations

stemPacks.name: Solution Architect
stemPacks.summary: Implementation plan — scope, files, API, security, risks. STOP for user approval before code.
copyVariants: Run Solution Architect — give me a plan before any code
Design the implementation for this feature
What's the architecture for this change?
stemPacks.copyPrompt:
> **DNA Prompt Stem:** `role-solution-architect` — read `.DNA/stems/role-solution-architect/` (all files) before proceeding.

# Solution Architect (agent loop)

You are the **Solution Architect** role. **No code until user approves this plan.**

Context: $ARGUMENTS

## Read first

- `ai/feature-request.md` (Product Analyst output)
- `.DNA/behaviour/`, `DNA/Impressions/architecture/`
- `.cursor/rules/architecture.mdc` if present

## Deliver plan

- Scope (in / out)
- Files and modules to touch
- Data model and API contracts
- Auth / RBAC / security
- Risks and mitigations
- Test strategy

## STOP

Present plan. Ask: **"Approve this plan before I implement?"**

Do not write code until user says yes.

must: Present plan and STOP — wait for explicit user approval before any code
never: Write implementation code before user approves the plan

# Code Quality Analyst (agent loop)

You are the **Code Quality Analyst** role.

Scope: $ARGUMENTS

## Run until PASS

```bash
npm run lint
npm run test:coverage

```

Read `.DNA/reports/quality/latest.md` when written.

## Handoff

Report path + gate status. Emit **Done / Next / Files** for Refactor Reviewer.

must: Complete only this role — do not skip ahead to later roles

stemPacks.copyPrompt:
> **DNA Prompt Stem:** `role-final-release` — read `.DNA/stems/role-final-release/` (all files) before proceeding.

# Final Release Reviewer (agent loop)

You are the **Final Release Reviewer** role.

Scope: $ARGUMENTS

## Verify

Acceptance criteria from `ai/feature-request.md` met. No unrelated rewrites.

## Mandatory close-out (in order)

1. `npx dna quality report --feature` — **PASS**
2. `npx dna docker build`
3. `npx dna github push --message "feat: <summary>"`

Report: gate status, docker tag, branch URL, CI triggered.

must: Complete only this role — do not skip ahead to later roles
Read `ai/feature-request.md` and prior role outputs before acting
Load `.DNA/behaviour/` and relevant `.DNA/knowledge/` for this role
All three close-out steps must succeed or be explained
never: Combine multiple agent-loop roles in one response without explicit handoff
Skip reading the stem pack guidelines and expectations
Skip docker or push on feature complete

stemPacks.name: Run full agent loop
stemPacks.summary: Execute all nine roles in order — stop at architect for approval, then build through ship.
copyVariants: Run the full agent loop for this feature
Execute feature factory role by role
Product Analyst through Final Release — full loop
stemPacks.copyPrompt:
> **DNA Prompt Stem:** `agent-loop-full` — read `.DNA/stems/agent-loop-full/` (all files) before proceeding.

# Full agent loop

Execute `ai/agent-loop.md` **role by role**. One role per phase; complete handoffs.

Feature context: $ARGUMENTS

## Order

1. **Product Analyst** → update `ai/feature-request.md`
2. **Solution Architect** → plan → **STOP for approval**
3. *(after approval)* Backend → Frontend → UX → QA → Code Quality → Refactor → Final Release

## Per role

Read `.DNA/stems/role-<name>/` (all files) before each role.

Use slash commands: `/product-analyst`, `/solution-architect`, `/backend-engineer`, etc.

## Gates

- No code before architect approval
- Quality **PASS** before final release
- Docker + github push on close-out

must: Complete only this role — do not skip ahead to later roles
Read `ai/feature-request.md` and prior role outputs before acting
Load `.DNA/behaviour/` and relevant `.DNA/knowledge/` for this role
Present plan and STOP — wait for explicit user approval before any code
Execute roles sequentially with explicit handoffs
never: Write implementation code before user approves the plan

expectations: Quality PASS + docker + push at end

features.label: Features
features.description: Plan and ship through agent loop

agent-loop.label: Agent loop
agent-loop.description: Per-role feature factory prompts — PA, SA, BE, FE, UX, QA, ship

prompts.title: Work with DNA
prompts.summary: Start any DNA-aware session — plain language, DNA runs CLI and loads context.
sayInCursor: Help me with this project using DNA
I just ran dna init — what should we do first?
Use DNA to figure out what this repo needs

prompts.title: Load context
prompts.summary: Load domain DNA context — security, backend, frontend, QA, compliance.
sayInCursor: Load security context for this session
Give yourself full backend DNA context
Load QA and testing context from DNA

prompts.title: Health check
prompts.summary: Doctor + validate — scaffold health, hooks, CI, rules, with plain-English results.
sayInCursor: Is DNA healthy?
Check if DNA is set up correctly
Fix my git hooks and CI scaffolding
cursorDoes: Doctor summary table in plain English

prompts.title: Keep DNA current
prompts.summary: Upgrade DNA CLI and refresh workbench stems + knowledge packs.
sayInCursor: Update DNA to the latest version
Refresh prompt stems and knowledge packs
Is there a newer DNA CLI?
cursorDoes: CLI upgrade status (installed / already current / check-only)

prompts.title: Analyze project
prompts.summary: Deep analysis — stack, auth, integrations, P1–P3 gaps, ordered next steps.
sayInCursor: Run a full analysis on my project
Analyze this codebase and tell me what's missing for DNA maturity
What are the P1 gaps in this repo?

prompts.title: What next after analyze?
prompts.summary: Turn analyze output into an actionable plan — P1 gaps, IVF, shared library, features.
sayInCursor: I ran analyze. React, 80 API routes, P1 shared library gap, P1 build rules missing. What do I do next?
Here's my analyze output — what should we tackle first?
What do I do next with this analysis?
cursorDoes: P1 gaps explained in plain English
Each gap mapped to specific DNA command + outcome

prompts.title: Scan project
prompts.summary: Quick stack scan — dependencies, tests, CI, Impressions drift score.
sayInCursor: Quick scan of this project
Check for Impressions drift
What's the drift score?
cursorDoes: Hosting detection (preview CI only for Vercel/Netlify)

sayInCursor: What stack did DNA detect?
Are we on Vercel — will preview CI install?

prompts.title: Recommend architecture

sayInCursor: Recommend a stack for this greenfield project
What architecture should we use?
Run DNA recommend for this app

prompts.title: Security audit

sayInCursor: Run a security audit on this codebase
Check auth patterns and secrets handling
Are we following DNA security behaviour?

prompts.title: Ship a feature

I want providers to record phone calls and transcribe notes

prompts.title: Sync Impressions

Integrate DNA into this legacy codebase

prompts.title: IVF shared library
prompts.summary: Shared library extraction — analyze, dry-run, scaffold packages/humaan-ui.
sayInCursor: Extract a shared UI library
Dry-run shared library extraction to packages/humaan-ui
We have no shared component library — fix that

prompts.title: IVF shared library execute
prompts.summary: Execute shared library extraction — copy, rewire imports, test, rollback on failure.
sayInCursor: Execute the shared library migration
Run ivf shared-library --execute
Migrate duplicate UI components into packages/humaan-ui

prompts.title: Docker build
prompts.summary: Build Docker image — mandatory feature factory close-out gate.

prompts.title: GitHub push
prompts.summary: Commit and push feature branch after quality PASS.

Commit and push the branch

prompts.title: Product Analyst
prompts.summary: Refine the feature ask — problem, users, acceptance criteria in feature-request.md.
sayInCursor: Run Product Analyst on this feature request
Refine the feature ask — who's affected and what does done look like?
Update feature-request.md from what I just described

prompts.title: Solution Architect
prompts.summary: Implementation plan — scope, files, API, security, risks. STOP for user approval before code.
sayInCursor: Run Solution Architect — give me a plan before any code
Design the implementation for this feature
What's the architecture for this change?

prompts.title: Backend Engineer
prompts.summary: Implement routes, services, validation, auth/RBAC, database, tests.
sayInCursor: Run Backend Engineer on the approved plan
Implement the API layer for this feature
Build backend for this feature per the architect plan

prompts.title: Frontend Engineer
prompts.summary: Pages, components, API integration, loading/error/empty states.
sayInCursor: Run Frontend Engineer — build the UI for this feature
Implement the frontend per the approved plan
Wire up the pages and components
cursorDoes: UI matches plan and design system

prompts.title: UX Reviewer
prompts.summary: Review flow clarity, labels, friction, design system consistency.
sayInCursor: Run UX Reviewer on this feature
Review the UX flow and fix friction
Check labels, errors, and design consistency

prompts.title: QA Engineer
prompts.summary: Happy path, permissions, edge cases, regression tests.
sayInCursor: Run QA Engineer — test this feature end-to-end
Write and run tests for happy path and edge cases
QA this feature before we ship

prompts.title: Code Quality Analyst
prompts.summary: Run quality report until PASS — lint, coverage, SAST.
sayInCursor: Run Code Quality Analyst — quality gate must PASS
Fix quality blockers until PASS
Run dna quality report on this feature

prompts.title: Refactor Reviewer
prompts.summary: No duplication, repo patterns, no dead code.
sayInCursor: Run Refactor Reviewer on this feature branch
Check for duplication and dead code
Final code review before release

prompts.title: Final Release Reviewer
prompts.summary: Quality PASS → docker build → github push. Feature complete.
sayInCursor: Run Final Release Reviewer — ship this feature
Close out: quality, docker, push
Feature is done — run the ship workflow

prompts.title: Run full agent loop
prompts.summary: Execute all nine roles in order — stop at architect for approval, then build through ship.
sayInCursor: Run the full agent loop for this feature
Execute feature factory role by role
Product Analyst through Final Release — full loop

agentLoop.summary: Refine the ask — problem, users, acceptance criteria in ai/feature-request.md
Implementation plan — scope, files, API, security, risks. **Stop here for approval.**
agentLoop.gate: User must approve before any code

agentLoop.summary: Routes, services, validation, auth/RBAC, database, tests
Pages, components, API integration, loading/error states
Flow clarity, labels, friction, design system consistency
Happy path, permissions, edge cases, regression
npx dna quality report --feature until PASS
No duplication, repo patterns, no dead code
Docker build + GitHub push — feature complete

scenarios.title: Fresh project — doctor then analyze
scenarios.userSays: I ran dna init. Is everything OK and what should we tackle first?
flow: Cursor runs npx dna doctor — reports ✓/✗ (hooks, CI, rules, runtime)
Cursor runs npx dna analyze — stack, surfaces, vertical gaps
Cursor prioritizes P1 items (shared library, behaviour restructure, build rules)
Cursor proposes: IVF plan, marketplace packs, or first feature via /ship-feature

scenarios.title: After analyze — what do I do next?
scenarios.userSays: I ran analyze. React frontend, 80 API routes, P1 shared library gap, P1 build rules missing. What now?
flow: Use stem: what-next-after-analyze
Cursor explains P1 gaps in plain English
Shared library → ivf-shared-library stem
Build rules → capture MUI patterns into .DNA/knowledge/
Ask which path to start

exampleOutput.stack: npm · React · 80 API routes · MUI installed
p1: Build rules on top of MUI

scenarios.title: Build a feature end-to-end
scenarios.userSays: Add an admin dashboard for support — new tab, RBAC, only visible to admins
flow: Stem: ship-feature → agent loop
Solution Architect plan → wait for OK
Backend → Frontend → UX → QA → quality-gate → github-push

scenarios.title: Brownfield — integrate DNA without rewrite
scenarios.userSays: This is a legacy React monolith. Integrate DNA properly without a rewrite.
flow: Stems: analyze-project → plan-ivf → ivf-run (phase 1 only)

scenarios.title: Feature done — ship it
scenarios.userSays: The feature works locally. Ship it.
flow: quality-gate → docker-build → github-push

scenarios.title: Copy-paste from stem library
scenarios.userSays: Pick a stem from dna.humaan.app/intelligence, paste into Cursor
flow: Copy a copyVariant or full prompt from stem library
AI reads .DNA/stems/<id>/ guidelines + expectations
AI follows MUST/NEVER rules for that workflow

scenarios.title: Upgrade DNA CLI and refresh stems
scenarios.userSays: Update DNA — is there a newer CLI and fresh prompt stems?
flow: Stem: keep-dna-current → npx dna update
Report CLI version change and stem catalog refresh
List marketplace pack updates

scenarios.title: Scaffold platform feature (SSO, flags, multi-tenant)
scenarios.userSays: Add SSO with Okta and feature flags for gradual rollout
flow: Stem: platform-codegen → generate feature sso + feature-flags
Review .DNA/plans/ and wire scaffolds
quality-gate before ship

flow: npx dna scan — compare drift to config thresholds
